Tests for the `toPrecision` method on numbers, including edge cases and error handling.

# Purpose
This code is a test suite for the JavaScript `Number.prototype.toPrecision` method, which formats a number to a specified precision. The tests verify that the method returns the expected string representation of numbers when different precision values are applied. The code includes assertions to check the behavior of `toPrecision` with various numbers, including positive and negative numbers, zero, `Infinity`, `-Infinity`, and `NaN`. It also tests edge cases, such as when the precision is not an integer or is outside the valid range, ensuring that the method throws the appropriate `RangeError` or `TypeError`. The comment notes that the tests may not pass on systems using `FLOAT32` due to precision issues.
